ground_truth.title: Stanford Computer Science Grads Find Their Degrees No Longer Guarantee Jobs
ground_truth.author: BeauHD
ground_truth.main_content: Elite computer science degrees are no longer a guaranteed on-ramp to tech jobs, as AI-driven coding tools slash demand for entry-level engineers and concentrate hiring around a small pool of already 'elite' or AI-savvy developers. 'I think that's crazy.' While the rapidly advancing coding capabilities of generative AI have made experienced engineers more productive, they have also hobbled the job prospects of early-career software engineers. Stanford students describe a suddenly skewed job market, where just a small slice of graduates -- those considered 'cracked engineers' who already have thick resumes building products and doing research -- are getting the few good jobs, leaving everyone else to fight for scraps. 'There's definitely a very dreary mood on campus,' said a recent computer science graduate who asked not to be named so they could speak freely. 'People [who are] job hunting are very stressed out, and it's very hard for them to actually secure jobs.' The shake-up is being felt across California colleges, including UC Berkeley, USC and others. A common sentiment from hiring managers is that where they previously needed ten engineers, they now only need 'two skilled engineers and one of these LLM-based agents,' which can be just as productive, said Nenad Medvidovic, a computer science professor at the University of Southern California. 'The AI now can code better than the average junior developer that comes out of the best schools out there.'
with: Elite computer science degrees are no longer a guaranteed on-ramp to tech jobs, as AI-driven coding tools slash demand for entry-level engineers.
The rapidly advancing coding capabilities of generative AI have made experienced engineers more productive, they have also hobbled the job prospects of early-career software engineers.
A common sentiment from hiring managers is that where they previously needed ten engineers, they now only need 'two skilled engineers and one of these LLM-based agents.'
We don't need the junior developers anymore. The AI now can code better than the average junior developer that comes out of the best schools out there.
